---
title: "R Episode 13 — Reshaping Data with tidyr"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Objectives
By the end of this episode you will be able to:

- Explain what makes data "tidy."
- Reshape between long and wide with `pivot_longer()` and `pivot_wider()`.
- Combine reshaping with dplyr in a pipeline.

**Teaching:** 35 min · **Exercises:** 25 min
:::

## Tidy data

Data is **tidy** when: each variable is a column, each observation is a row, and each value is a
cell. Tidy data is what dplyr and ggplot2 expect, so most analysis starts with reshaping into
tidy form.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Instructor note
The original Carpentries lesson used `gather()`/`spread()`. Those are **superseded** — teach
`pivot_longer()`/`pivot_wider()`, which are clearer and actively maintained. Mention the old
names only so learners recognize them in legacy code.
:::

## Wide → long with pivot_longer()

Suppose GDP per capita is spread across year columns (`gdp_1997`, `gdp_2002`, `gdp_2007`):

```r
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

long <- wide |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("gdp_"),
    names_to = "year",
    names_prefix = "gdp_",
    values_to = "gdpPercap"
  )
long
```

## Long → wide with pivot_wider()

The inverse — spread one column of names across multiple columns:

```r
wide_again <- long |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = year,
    names_prefix = "gdp_",
    values_from = gdpPercap
  )
```

## Reshape, then analyze

Reshaping is usually a step inside a larger pipeline:

```r
gapminder |>
  filter(continent == "Europe") |>
  select(country, year, lifeExp) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = year, values_from = lifeExp)
```

…producing a country-by-year matrix of life expectancy, ready for a table or export.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Challenge — Tidy then summarize
Starting from a wide table `wide` with `gdp_1997`/`gdp_2002`/`gdp_2007`, pivot to long form and
compute the mean GDP per capita per year.

### Solution

```r
wide |>
  pivot_longer(starts_with("gdp_"), names_to = "year",
               names_prefix = "gdp_", values_to = "gdp") |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(mean_gdp = mean(gdp), .groups = "drop")
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Key points
- Tidy = one variable per column, one observation per row.
- `pivot_longer()` gathers columns into rows; `pivot_wider()` does the reverse.
- These replace the superseded `gather()`/`spread()`.
- Reshaping is usually one step in a dplyr pipeline.
:::

Next: [Episode 14 — Producing Reports with Quarto](r-14-quarto-reports.qmd).
